tracks.name: Data Structures & Algorithms
tracks.description: The Striver A2Z (takeUforward) sheet, end to end — 18 steps and 50+ topic blocks carrying you from language syntax and sorting through arrays, binary search, linked lists, recursion & backtracking, stacks/queues, trees, graphs, DP, tries and advanced strings. You don't just solve once: every problem becomes a card you re-solve on an FSRS schedule, so patterns (sliding window, two pointers, binary-search-on-answer, DP-on-subsequences) stay reflexive for interviews. Outcome: confident, pattern-first problem solving on any medium/hard prompt.

materials.title: Striver A2Z sheet — official syllabus
materials.notes:
WATCH (~15 min skim)
• Striver A2Z sheet — official syllabus
• Focus: Bookmark. Single source of truth for the 450.

DO (this block)
1. Read/skim with a pen — don't passively scroll.
2. Write 3 bullet takeaways in your own words.
3. Connect one idea to something you already know in this track.

DELIVERABLE
• 3 takeaway bullets in your notes app

RECALL (after Reveal)
• Summarize Striver A2Z sheet — official syllabus in 60 seconds without notes.

materials.title: Star patterns (15+ classics)
materials.notes:
WATCH (~3 min read)
• Open the problem: Star patterns (15+ classics)
• Read constraints twice. Note input size → required complexity.
• Pattern: Pyramids, half-pyramids, hollow, diamond. Build loop intuition.

DO (this block — no solution peeking)
1. Write the approach in plain English (2–3 sentences).
2. State time and space complexity before coding.
3. Code from scratch. Run at least 2 tests including one edge case.
4. If stuck >15 min, read ONE hint only, then retry.

DELIVERABLE (before you rate Good)
• Accepted submission (green check on LeetCode/GFG)
• One-line pattern tag saved in your notes

RECALL (after Reveal — no notes)
• Explain the Star patterns (15+ classics) pattern in 30 seconds.
• Name one problem where this same pattern applies.

tracks.name: Mathematics for AI
tracks.description: The mathematical bedrock under all of machine learning, built on the free *Mathematics for Machine Learning* (Deisenroth, Faisal & Ong) and paired with 3Blue1Brown and StatQuest for visual intuition. Ten chapters move from linear algebra and analytic geometry → matrix decompositions → vector calculus → probability → optimization, then land on two worked applications: linear regression and PCA. The densest, most foundational track (cognitive ×1.4) — outcome: you can read an ML paper's math and know *why* the gradients flow the way they do.

materials.title: StatQuest Stats · Session 1: Mean, median, mode, histograms (eps 1-6)
materials.notes:
WATCH (~58 min video)

tracks.name: LLM & Machine Learning
tracks.description: A bottom-up path from classical ML to frontier systems, anchored by Andrej Karpathy's Zero-to-Hero builds plus canonical papers and books. You go: scikit-learn fundamentals → neural nets and backprop from scratch → PyTorch/fast.ai → transformers and LLM pre/post-training → alignment, RAG and agents (incl. MCP) → diffusion, MoE, reasoning and multimodal models → production, evals and MLOps. Heavy on *building* (micrograd, makemore, nanoGPT) over watching. Outcome: you can train, fine-tune, ground and ship a modern model end to end.

materials.title: Google — Machine Learning Crash Course
materials.notes: 15-hour fast-paced intro: loss, gradient descent, overfitting, regularization, feature crosses, embeddings, fairness. With Colab labs.

materials.title: Microsoft — Machine Learning for Beginners
materials.notes: 12-week classic ML curriculum with scikit-learn. Useful complement to StatQuest's theory.

materials.title: scikit-learn — User Guide
materials.notes:
WATCH (~15 min skim)
• scikit-learn — User Guide
• Focus: The canonical Python ML toolbox. Skim sections 1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 9 — these are the patterns you'll re-use forever.

DO (this block)
1. Read/skim with a pen — don't passively scroll.
2. Write 3 bullet takeaways in your own words.
3. Connect one idea to something you already know in this track.

DELIVERABLE
• 3 takeaway bullets in your notes app

RECALL (after Reveal)
• Summarize scikit-learn — User Guide in 60 seconds without notes.

materials.notes: Deeper than the blitz: datasets/dataloaders, transforms, save/load, TensorBoard. Set baseline fluency.

materials.title: Fast.ai — Practical Deep Learning for Coders v5
materials.notes: Top-down approach: build CNN classifiers + tabular + NLP before theory. Jeremy Howard. 9 lessons.

materials.title: Andrew Ng — Deep Learning Specialization
materials.notes: 5 courses: NN basics → hyperparameters → ML projects → CNN → sequence models. Slow but bulletproof foundation.

materials.title: StatQuest NN · Session 1: Forward pass, activations, softmax (eps 1-5)
materials.notes:
WATCH (~55 min video)
• Open playlist at episode 1. Watch eps 1-5 (5 videos).
• How data flows forward; activation roles

DO (during this 2h block)
1. Pause after each video; write one sentence in your own words.
2. Sketch or table the key idea — don't just highlight the video.
3. Note one connection to prior material in this track.

DELIVERABLE (before you rate Good)
• 5 one-line summary(ies) — one per video
• One diagram, table, or formula sheet in your notes app

RECALL (after Reveal — no notes)
• Explain “Forward pass, activations, softmax” in 60 seconds without notes.
• What's the #1 mistake beginners make here?

tracks.name: System Design
tracks.description: How real systems are designed, scaled and operated — low-level design patterns → distributed building blocks (caching, sharding, queues, consensus, CAP) → full HLD case studies (URL shortener, news feed, chat, rate limiter, payments) → and a modern GenAI-infra capstone (vector DBs, KV-cache, vLLM serving, inference scaling). From a parking-lot LLD whiteboard to designing the platform that serves a model in production. Outcome: structured, trade-off-driven answers to any design round.
